name: North-Mini-Code-1.0
tagline: Cohere's first agentic coding model — a 30B/3B-active MoE that's open under Apache 2.0.
seoTitle: Cohere North-Mini-Code-1.0 — Open Agentic Coding Model
metaDescription: North-Mini-Code-1.0 is Cohere's first agentic coding model: a 30B/3B-active MoE under Apache 2.0, with a 256K context and strong SWE-Bench scores.
overview: North-Mini-Code-1.0 is Cohere's first agentic coding model and, per Cohere, the inaugural member of its next generation of models. It was released on June 9, 2026 under the Apache 2.0 license, with open weights available on Hugging Face and via Cohere's Model Vault.

Architecturally it is a sparse Mixture-of-Experts decoder-only Transformer with 30B total parameters and roughly 3B active per token. It supports a 256K-token context window with up to 64K tokens of generation, and is trained with tool-use and interleaved thinking for agent-based coding workflows.

Cohere positions the model for efficiency, citing up to 2.8x higher output throughput than Devstral Small 2 and a 30% advantage in inter-token latency, alongside an Artificial Analysis Coding Index score of 33.4.
strengths: Open weights under permissive Apache 2.0 license
Efficient 3B-active MoE for high throughput and low latency
Built-in tool use and interleaved thinking for agent workflows
useCases: Agentic software engineering and code generation

faq.a: Yes. Cohere released North-Mini-Code-1.0 under the Apache 2.0 license with open weights available on Hugging Face (CohereLabs) and through Cohere's Model Vault. The permissive license allows commercial use, self-hosting, and customization, making it suitable for private deployments of agentic coding assistants.

It is a sparse Mixture-of-Experts model with 30B total parameters and roughly 3B active per token. This MoE design lets it deliver high output throughput and low latency — Cohere cites up to 2.8x the throughput of Devstral Small 2 — while keeping inference cost down relative to dense models of similar quality.

faq.q: What benchmarks does North-Mini-Code-1.0 score on?
faq.a: Per Cohere's blog, North-Mini-Code-1.0 reaches 83.2% pass@1 on SWE-Bench Verified, 63.0% pass@1 on Terminal-Bench v2, and 61.0% pass@1 on Mini-SWE-Agent. Cohere also reports an Artificial Analysis Coding Index score of 33.4, positioning it as competitive among similarly sized coding models.

It is Cohere's first agentic coding model, optimized for code generation, agentic software engineering, and terminal tasks. It is trained with tool-use and interleaved thinking for agent-based workflows, supports a 256K-token context with up to 64K-token generation, and targets latency-sensitive developer tooling.

versionHistory.note: Cohere's first agentic coding model; 30B MoE, Apache 2.0
Scalable enterprise LLM; advanced RAG + tool use
